methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $60M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Rainmaker
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Dronamics
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Intramotev
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Seneca is a Series A drone and autonomous systems company founded by Stuart Landesberg and Nick Foley that has raised $60M to date. The broader Drones & Autonomous sector includes eight private peers (Zipline, Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air among others) but no public comparables, limiting traditional peer benchmarking precision.

Given the early-stage nature and sparse peer dataset at identical funding stage, the Stage-Progression method is the primary applicable valuation tool. Using $60M in total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage progression yields an implied value of $120M. The Peer-Median approach is not applicable; while five same-sector peers exist, none satisfy the narrow filtering criteria (same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier) required for reliable comparison, and using an all-sector median would introduce unwarranted noise.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $102M (low) to $138M (high), centered on $120M. This range reflects the limited data density supporting the estimate—the triangulation rests on a single defensible method rather than convergence across multiple approaches. Key uncertainties include the stability of typical step-up multipliers in the autonomous systems subsector, capital efficiency relative to stage-peer benchmarks, and progress metrics since the $60M raise. Refinement would require either additional Series A-stage or recent Series B exits in narrower drone/autonomous verticals.
